html:
“When placed, it allows the survivor to write a message that can be read by themselves or other survivors later.”

-

The Sign is a craftable Structure. The Sign requires 1 Board to craft and a Science Machine to prototype. If it is destroyed, the Boards are returned, allowing the player to build a new Sign elsewhere.

Signs can be written on after being placed, much like the Directional Signs. When writing on a sign, the dialog will give the option to cancel, generate random text, or write to the sign. The text box can also be selected and typed into. There is no restriction on who can write or what can be written on them, but they can only be written on once. When examined, the character whose player examined it will say what is written on the sign. When the sign is burnt, the character will say something about it being burnt, thus losing its written information.

Alongside being able to display any written text, Signs show up as an icon on the Map. As such, it can be used as a marker for areas of interest which do not show up on the Map (such as Blue Mushrooms and/or Mushroom Rings, or Beefalo herds).

Trivia

  • Signs were added in A Little Rain Must Fall update.
  • One can craft Signs for free by clicking the Boards and then crafting a Sign while having the boards still selected.
  • In Wolfgang's line for the Codex Umbra, he says he cannot read. Despite this, he is still able to read out what is written on a Sign in-game.

Gallery

  • A Sign marking a Mushroom ring.
  • Maxwell's Tooth Traps and a couple of world-spawned Signs.
  • Old Crafting Menu Sign Icon
  • Sign Burnt

“It says 'You are here'.”
–Wilson
“Less reading! More burning!”
–Willow
“Is no time for reading signs!”
–Wolfgang
“A mark is made here, however transient...”
–Wendy
“YOU ARE HERE”
–WX-78
“A navigational aid.”
–Wickerbottom
“Is there something wood can't do?”
–Woodie
“It says... nothing.”
–Maxwell
“A möst well placed sign.”
–Wigfrid
“We could write on that if only we had a pen!”
–Webber
“What's the use in a sign around here?”
–Warly
“Words on friends”
–Wormwood
“I'll take this as a sign.”
–Winona
“"You are here" it says, it says.”
–Wortox
“What it say?”
–Wurt
“Someone has bad handwriting.”
–Walter
“That must be some kind of sign.”
–Wanda

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Dấu hiệu là một cấu trúc có thể chế tạo được. Biển hiệu yêu cầu 1 Bảng để chế tạo và Máy Khoa học để tạo nguyên mẫu. Nếu nó bị phá hủy, các Bảng sẽ được trả lại, cho phép người chơi xây dựng một Biển hiệu mới ở nơi khác.

{{Scrapbook_Info|When placed, it allows the survivor to write a message that can be read by themselves or other survivors later.}}
The '''Sign''' is a craftable [[Structure]]. The Sign requires 1 [[Board]] to craft and a [[Science Machine]] to prototype. If it is destroyed, the [[Boards]] are returned, allowing the player to build a new Sign elsewhere.

Signs can be written on after being placed, much like the [[Directional Sign]]s. When writing on a sign, the dialog will give the option to cancel, generate random text, or write to the sign. The text box can also be selected and typed into. There is no restriction on who can write or what can be written on them, but they can only be written on once. When examined, the character whose player examined it will say what is written on the sign. When the sign is burnt, the character will say something about it being burnt, thus losing its written information.

Alongside being able to display any written text, Signs show up as an icon on the [[Map]]. As such, it can be used as a marker for areas of interest which do not show up on the Map (such as [[Blue Mushroom]]s and/or Mushroom Rings, or [[Beefalo]] herds).

== {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Signs were added in ''[[Version History|A Little Rain Must Fall]]'' update.
* One can craft Signs for free by clicking the Boards and then crafting a Sign while having the boards still selected.
* In [[Wolfgang]]'s line for the [[Codex Umbra/DST|Codex Umbra]], he says he cannot read. Despite this, he is still able to read out what is written on a Sign in-game.
